author: @blackcoalboy
text: Okay i have an idea that would save you a lot of Money , Skip the competition and mail it straight to ME, Job done.

author: @rocker-barrel4786
text: I sent my polumo back its huge way to big for me at 5ft 6. Such a shame.its such a great bit of kit,im gutted tbh. Thst digital screen gubbins doesnt come mph version only european thus far.

author: @andrewradford3953
text:
An ebike is the closest thing to teleportation!
Since getting sciatica I can't walk more than 200m before loosing feeling in my legs, but my ebike gets me anywhere on my 20 acre farm in minutes at up to 25km/h.
As an Australian I think it's fair to limit the competition to an affordable postage range. Maybe it could include Scotland or Wales, maybe not.

author: @zacharysimpson7353
text: I'd help with that for you

replies.author: @zacharysimpson7353
replies.text: Information of everything so i can  print it at a library.

author: @bettyswallocks6411
text: I wonder how many African Swallows would be needed to transport the package? And what would be the air speed velocity of the optimum number of swallows?

author: @andrewharpin6749
text:
The ones you've tried so far a wheel motor, I assume cadence sensor.

The hub motor versions tend to use a torque sensor, which is much faster to respond to your pedalling and respond with appropriate torque (if tuned correctly). 

They are obviously the more expensive versions, but a different a better experience IMHO.

Eskute do a hub drive version. Maybe an option for the next review.

author: @kennyclement2823
text:
Oklahoma USA, at this time $5 gasoline, I'm really interested in the e-bikes, but in our hilly windy country and long distances, I believe a mountain back would be more in line with my need, but not my budget!
I think I'm going to go through my junk file and see what I can come up with

author: @kylewellman402
text: I cant speak for everyone, but i imagine nobody would really be upset or complain if you relied on us (the winner) to cover the shipping cost. You already paid for the bike and are kind enough to give it away, it would just seem greedy to expect you to pay for shipping cost as well. That being said, id love to be a part of the competition. What do i need to do?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I guess if they didn't want to do that them they just wouldn't enter - nice one mate

replies.author: @kylewellman402
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering on an unrelated note, thank you for all the time and information you share. I have spent many years of my life now self teaching ways to generate electricity, studying physics and the periodic table, coming up with potential prototypes for fuelless energy generation ( i havent built a prototype yet  but i really think im on to something). A couple of my hypothesis that would be required for my concept to work have actually been proven true recently by different labs. Damn shame i couldn't have patented the ideas lol. But, to make an already long post way shorter, you have taught me a lot through your videos on how to store the energy generated. Different motor ideas and ways to get mechanical motion. Hell you taught me how to make rubies and it has been a little experiment at home that have gotten my step kids and son fascinated with experiments like that. In short, thank you sir for all you do, and all you share.

author: @synchro-dentally1965
text: The winner paying for shipping makes sense. I'd recommend providing some estimates for shipping rates to various places so competitors have an idea of what to expect. That way the winner wouldn't be caught by surprise.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's a good idea mate - like

author: @bobdeath5508
text: a simple competition where  the competitor pays shipping, I consider it acceptable

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate

author: @peterclark6290
text:
Make it a lottery. 
Anyone who enters the competition antes up; if enough enter then, the draw is recorded, and the shipping costs come from the subs. They who do enter get to be included in your patron channel for a short while.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I probably won't make it a lottery - but I really like the idea of adding in the shipping cost as an idea of what I might cost - nice one ate

author: @orpheuscreativeco9236

replies.text: that sounds pretty awesome mate - I am a fan of these because of the last mile impact they have and you have given me a nice idea right there - cheers

author: @D-B-Cooper
text: Around here everyone use to have a surfboard on their car, then a kayak, now an ebike. Soon they will break down and the dumpsters will be full of them. From the packaging, tires and battery, all I see is pollution.

replies.author: @cerealtiller
replies.text: The 'Black Belt Barrister' is Overpraising E Scooters on his Channel...seems to be a trend..Oh Well.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it depends - if they turn out to be a fad then yes you are right - if folks change their ways a bit then - no I would disagree - it's really up to us to make a change and dump the car and keep the bike - wouldn't you say?

author: @davidraper9629
text: Winner arranges owwn collection & carriage.

replies.author: @Coxeysbodgering
replies.text:
If local enough and acceptable to yourselves collection, decide which bike you want to give away, box it, measure and weigh it so that entrees can calculate shipping to them. 
Rule 1 you must arrange shipping and taxes (if applicable) at your own cost.
Rule 2 shipping must be arranged with in X days of the winner announced. If not it will be transferred to the runner up.
Rule 2
